13365.history: Served with the Royal Canadian Navy from completion on 11 August 1944. Returned to the Royal Navy on 20 June 1945. Transferred to the Royal New Zealand Navy on 3 september 1948 being renamed HMNZS Pukaki. Sold to be broken up for scrap in 1965. Scrapped in 1966.

13369.history: Served with the Royal Canadian Navy from completion on 2 August 1944. Returned to the Royal Navy on 20 June 1945. Transferred to the Royal New Zealand Navy on 1 April 1949 and renamed Tutira. Sold to be broken up for scrap in 1961. Scrapped in 1966.

13414.built_by: Swan Hunter and Wigham Richardson Ltd. (Wallsend-on-Tyne, U.K.)
13414.history: Completed as merchantile ferry Hampton Ferry in November 1934 Requisitioned by the Admiralty on 24 August 1939. Displacement: 2839 tons. Lenght: 347 feet Machinery: 4 boilers, two shafts, Parsons turbines, SHP 4800 = 16.5 knots. Armament: 1 4" AA gun, 2 .303" AA (2x1), 270 mines. To the Ministery of War Transport (MoWT) in 1940.
commanders: Capt. (retired) George Herbert Freyberg, OBE, RN (1 Sep 1939 - mid 1940)

13415.built_by: Swan Hunter and Wigham Richardson Ltd. (Wallsend-on-Tyne, U.K.)
13415.history: Completed as merchantile ferry Shepperton Ferry in March 1935 Requisitioned by the Admiralty on 24 August 1939. Displacement: 2839 tons. Lenght: 347 feet Machinery: 4 boilers, two shafts, Parsons turbines, SHP 4800 = 16.5 knots. Armament: 1 4" AA gun, 2 .303" AA (2x1), 270 mines. To the Ministery of War Transport (MoWT) in 1940.

13416.history: Completed as merchantile in July 1939 Requisitioned by the Admiralty on 10 September 1939. Displacement: 2197 tons. Lenght: 310 feet Machinery: Two shafts, Diesel engines, SHP 6160 = 19 knots. Armament: 1 4" gun, 2 12pdr AA guns (2x1), 244 mines. HMS Princess Victoria was mined and sunk off the mouth of the Humber on 19 May 1940.
commanders: Lt.Cdr. Paul Morrison Bushe Chavasse, RN (25 Sep 1939 - 1 Nov 1939)
Capt. John Buller Edward Hall, RN (1 Nov 1939 - 19 May 1940 (+))

13417.built_by: John Redhead & Sons Ltd. (South Shields, England, U.K.)
13417.history: Completed as merchantile in February 1938. Requisitioned by the Admiralty on 2 November 1939. Displacement: 5087 tons. Lenght: 424 feet Machinery: 2 boilers, one shaft, reciprocating engine, IHP 2620 = 15 knots. Complement: 105. Armament: 1 4" gun, 12pdr AA gun, 4 .5" AA (2x2), 280 mines. Returned on 29 March 1946.
commanders: Cdr. (retired) Robert Douglas King-Harman, DSC, RN (28 Sep 1939 - 15 Jun 1942)
Capt. (Retd.) Edward Clifford Watson, RN (15 Jun 1942 - Aug 1944)

13418.built_by: Joseph L. Thompson & Sons (Sunderland, England, U.K.)
13418.history: Requisitioned by the Admiralty while building on 25 November 1939. Displacement: 5936 tons. Lenght: 451 feet Machinery: 1 Shaft, Diesel engine, BHP 6700 = 16 knots. Armament: 2 4" AA guns (2x1), 2 2pdr AA (2x1) 2 .5" AA (1x2), 548 mines. Became repair ship Deer Sound (Penant: F 99) in 1944 Returned on 20 December 1947.
commanders: Capt. (Retd.) Edward Clifford Watson, RN (24 Apr 1940 - 25 May 1942)
A/Capt. (retired) Vernon Hammersley-Heenan, RN (25 May 1942 - late 1943)
A/Capt. Reginald Horace Johnson, DSC, RN (10 Dec 1944 - )

13419.built_by: Caledon Shipbuilding & Engineering Co. Ltd. (Dundee, Scotland, U.K.).
13419.history: Completed as merchantile in December 1929 Requisitioned by the Admiralty on 14 December 1939. Displacement: 7494 tons. Lenght: 460 feet Machinery: 2 Shafts, Diesel engine, BHP 4480 = 15.5 knots. Armament: 2 4" AA guns (2x1), 2 2pdr AA (2x1) 4 .5" AA (2x2), 410 mines. Became an amenties ship in 1944 for service with the British Pacific Fleet.
commanders: Capt. Wiliam Howard Dennis Friedberger, RN (15 Apr 1940 - 12 Feb 1941)
Capt. John Stuart Crawford, DSO, RN (12 Feb 1941 - 15 Jan 1942)
Capt. (retired) Rudolph Henry Fane de Salis, DSC, OBE, RN (15 Jan 1942 - late 1943)
A A Ferguson, RNR (late 1943 - 5 Nov 1944)
A/Capt. (retired) Henry Freston Nash, RN (5 Nov 1944 - early 1945)

13420.built_by: Workman, Clark & Co. Ltd. (Belfast, Northern Ireland)
13420.history: Completed as merchantile in September 1929 Requisitioned by the Admiralty on 30 December 1939. Displacement: 7593 tons. Lenght: 460 feet Machinery: 2 Shafts, Diesel engine, BHP 4480 = 15.5 knots. Armament: 3 4" AA guns (3x1), 2 2pdr AA (2x1) 4 .5" AA (2x2), 530 mines. Became an amenties ship in 1944 for service with the British Pacific Fleet. Returned on 26 April 1947.
commanders: Capt. (Retd.) Neville Brevoort Carey Brock, RN (1 May 1940 - 5 Nov 1940)
Cdr. George Anthony Francis Norfolk, RN (5 Nov 1940 - 21 Nov 1940)
Capt. (Retd.) Franklin Ratsey, RN (21 Nov 1940 - ca. Nov 43)
R N Mayo, RNVR (Ca. Nov 43 - 11 Nov 1944)
Lt.Cdr. Jack Collier, RNR (11 Nov 1944 - )

13421.history: Completed as merchantile in August 1929. Requisitioned by the Admiralty on 19 December 1939. Displacement: 11447 tons. Lenght: 496 feet Machinery: Two shafts, Diesel engine, SHP 7680 = 17 knots. Armament: 4" AA guns (2x1), 2 2pdr AA (2x1), 7 20mm AA (7x1), 4 .5" AA (2x2), 560 mines. Became and accomodation ship in 1945. Hit by U-boatDamaged on 26 Aug 1941 by U-652 (Fraatz).
commanders: Cdr. Alfred Mervyn Bingeman, RN (1 Apr 1940 - 17 Apr 1940)
A/Capt. Edward Murray Conrad Barraclough, RN (17 Apr 1940 - 11 Mar 1941)
Cdr. Charles Leslie Firth, RN (11 Mar 1941 - 19 May 1941)
A/Capt. John Creswell, RN (19 May 1941 - 15 Jul 1942)
A/Capt. John Hamilton Macnair, RN (15 Jul 1942 - Jan 1943)
A/Cdr. William Pope, DSO, RN (Jan 1943 - 2 Jun 1943)
A/Capt. John Hamilton Macnair, RN (2 Jun 1943 - 22 Nov 1943)
Capt. (retired) Rudolph Henry Fane de Salis, DSC, OBE, RN (22 Nov 1943 - 4 Nov 1944)
Lt. Reginald Alexander Norsworthy, RN (4 Nov 1944 - )

13422.history: Completed as merchantile in 1921. Requisitioned by the Admiralty on 9 June 1941. Displacement: 4636 tons. Lenght: 350 feet Machinery: Three boilers, two shafts, reciprocating engine, IHP 2700 = 15 knots. Armament: 2 4" guns (2x1), 240 mines. Bombed by Japanese aircraft 6 nautical miles North-West of Pompong Island (near Lingga) and scuttled on 14 February 1942.
commanders: Edward James Thomson, RNR (28 Feb 1941 - 14 Feb 1942)

13423.built_by: Caledon Shipbuilding & Engineering Co. Ltd. (Dundee, Scotland, U.K.).
13423.history: Completed as merchantile in May 1937 Requisitioned by the Admiralty in October 1940. Displacement: 7494 tons. Lenght: 357 feet Machinery: Two boilers, 1 shaft, reciprocating engine IHP 2500 = 11 knots. Armament: 2 4" guns (2x1), 2 2pdr AA (2x1) 8 .303" AA (8x1), 423 mines. Became an amenties ship in 1944 for service with the British Pacific Fleet.

13424.history: Arrived at Inverkeitihng to be scrapped in April 1969.
commanders: Lt.Cdr. Adelbert William Cust John Nicholson, RN (24 Sep 1937 - 21 Dec 1940)
Lt. Reginald Horace Johnson, RN (21 Dec 1940 - 17 Jan 1944)
Lt. Ralph Thomas White, RN (17 Jan 1944 - )

13425.history: Arrived at Dunston to be scrapped on 11 May 1964.
commanders: Lt. Richard Homewood, RN (27 Aug 1937 - 18 May 1944)
H A Inglis, RNR (18 May 1944 - mid/late45)

13426.built_by: Henry Robb Ltd. (Leith, U.K.) : Whites M.E.
13426.history: HMS Redstart was scuttled at Hong Kong on 19 December 1941 to prevent her capture by the Japanese
commanders: Lt. Alfred Frank Jackson, RN (Oct 1938 - Oct 1940)
Lt.Cdr. (retired) Henry Charles Sylvester Collingwood Selby, RN (Oct 1940 - 19 Dec 1941)

13427.built_by: Henry Robb Ltd. (Leith, U.K.) : Whites M.E.
commanders: Lt. Cyril Robert Pilgrim, RN (30 Sep 1938 - Jun 1941)
Lt.Cdr. Bertie Lucock Sadler, RN (Jun 1941 - 17 Mar 1942)
T/Lt. Francis Leslie Wilkins, RNR (17 Mar 1942 - 14 Jan 1943)
A S Holland, RNR (14 Jan 1943 - 9 Aug 1946)

13428.history: Renamed Miner I in 1942. Sold in 1967.
commanders: T/Lt. Robert Douglas Cooper, RNVR (24 Mar 1941 - 5 Apr 1943)
T/Lt. William Frank James Browning, RNVR (5 Apr 1943 - )

13429.history: Renamed Miner II in 1942. Expended as target on 18 March 1970.
commanders: T/Lt. John Pryse Edleston Thomas, RNVR (28 May 1941 - 15 May 1944)
T/Lt. Charles Cyril Inderwick, RNVR (15 May 1944 - 23 Jul 1945)
T/Lt. John Henry Kynman, RNVR (23 Jul 1945 - )

13430.history: Renamed Miner III in 1942. Scrapped in February 1972.
commanders: T/Lt. Leslie Arthur Martin, RNVR (15 Feb 1943 - early 1943)

13431.history: Renamed Miner IV in 1942. Scrapped in May 1964.
commanders: Percy Edgar Martin, RNR (18 Mar 1943 - 24 Jan 1944)
T/A/Lt.Cdr. Brian Raleigh Pedrette, RNVR (24 Jan 1944 - )

13432.history: Renamed Miner V in 1942. Expended as target on 6 June 1970.
commanders: D B Walton, RNVR (15 Feb 1943 - Mar 1943)
T/A/Lt.Cdr. A G Richman, RNR (Mar 1943 - 9 Aug 1943)
T/A/Lt.Cdr. Norman Baker, RNR (9 Aug 1943 - 14 May 1945)
T/A/Lt.Cdr. Ronald Hector Diaper, RNR (14 May 1945 - )
T/A/Lt.Cdr. Norman Baker, RNR (16 Feb 1942 - 9 Aug 1943)
T/A/Lt.Cdr. A G Richman, RNR (9 Aug 1943 - mid/late45)
T/Lt. Derrick Vernon Smith, RNR (19 Apr 1943 - 10 Sep 1945)
T/A/Lt.Cdr. John Carr, RNR (10 Sep 1945 - )
T/A/Lt.Cdr. Eric Charles Hoblyn, RNVR (3 Mar 1944 - )

13436.history: Destroyed incomplete on the stocks at Singapore in February 1942.

13437.built_by: Cochrane & Sons Shipbuilders Ltd. (Selby, U.K.) : Amos & Smith
13437.history: Arrived at Sunderland for scrapping on 9 May 1957.
commanders: T/Lt. Clement James Smith, RNVR (Dec 1942 - Mar 1944)
T/A/Lt.Cdr. Arthur Yarwood, RNR (Mar 1944 - Jun 1945)
L Plunnett, RCNVR (Jun 1945 - Apr 1946)

13438.built_by: Cochrane & Sons Shipbuilders Ltd. (Selby, U.K.) : Amos & Smith
13438.history: HMS Corncrake foundered in the North Atlantic in heavy weather on 25 January 1943.
commanders: T/A/Lt.Cdr. Lewis Russell Renfrew, RNR (late 1942 - 25 Jan 1943 (+))

13439.built_by: Cook, Welton & Gemmill (Beverley, U.K.) : C.D. Holmes (Hull, U.K.)
commanders: T/A/Lt.Cdr. Robert Douglas Cooper, RNVR (Jun 1943 - 21 Feb 1944)
T/Lt. Ronald Hector Diaper, RNR (21 Feb 1944 - 8 Dec 1944)
Percy Edgar Martin, RNR (8 Dec 1944 - early 1946)
